steps: Export only the columns you need from your CRM — gift amount, date, hour, premium, new-vs-renewing, channel. No names, emails, addresses, or phone numbers.
Put that stripped file in a folder on your computer called something like "drive-readout."
Open Claude Code in that folder and ask it to read the file and list back the columns it sees, before it does anything else.
Ask for the workbook you need: a summary tab, an hour-by-hour take, a new-vs-renewing split, and a comparison to your last drive.
Tell it to re-sum every column against the source file and show you that check before handing the workbook back.
Check the workbook's totals against your CRM's own totals for the drive before the numbers go anywhere near the board.
Save the prompt as a Skill, so the station owns this for next drive, not just you.
verify: Open the finished workbook and search it for an @ sign — if any email address survived the strip, it isn't safe to work with; start over from a clean export.
